Description of Risk: Limited availability of qualified contractors and materials, especially for heat pumps, insulation, windows, BACS, and PV; risk of cost inflation and delays.
Mitigation Measures: Medium-term skills and certification programmes; targeted training for key trades; framework contracts; advance procurement planning; market intelligence monitoring.

Description of Risk: Municipalities and homeowner associations may lack the expertise and staff needed to manage MEPS-scale renovation programmes, procurement, and oversight.
Mitigation Measures: OSS staffing and training; standardized templates for audits, design, procurement and HOA decision-making; technical helpdesks; dedicated support teams for complex HOAs.

Description of Risk: Lengthy permitting processes and slow DSO response times for PV and heat pump connections, especially under Article 10 solar-triggered works.
Mitigation Measures: Streamlined digital permitting workflows; single-entry systems; accelerated DSO/TSO coordination; fast-track grid connection procedures for MEPS-triggered installations; clear national service-level agreements.

Description of Risk: Risk of slow deployment or incomplete integration of EPC registry, BRP platform, programme MIS and DH/prosumer data into the Integrated Building Stock Database.
Mitigation Measures: Dedicated MDLPA digitalisation roadmap; interoperable data protocols; phased integration; performance monitoring; early testing and piloting with selected municipalities.

Risk Category: Social acceptance, affordability and split incentives
Description of Risk: Resistance in low-income or mixed-tenure buildings; landlords reluctant to invest; tenants concerned about rent increases; affordability constraints for vulnerable households.
Mitigation Measures: Social safeguards under Section 3.3; rent-cap frameworks; staged compliance via BRPs; higher aid intensities for vulnerable households; turnkey renovation pathways; landlord–tenant cost-sharing models.
